issue_title: Future Improvement: The transforms covered in The Great Time Series Classification BakeOff
issue_body:
There was an interesting paper last year which compared a bunch of newly published time series classifiers. 

http://www-bcf.usc.edu/~liu32/milets16/paper/MiLeTS_2016_paper_5.pdf

In a wild oversimplification, several of the classifiers simply transform the data in a new way, then run pretty standard time series classification algorithms on the transformed data. 

It would be awesome if tsfresh could become Python's central repository for these different time series data transformations. 

The cool part about this project is that they have all the source code available. The team from UEA even reached out to the authors of different papers if the code they produced didn't accurately recreate the results of the published papers. 

https://bitbucket.org/TonyBagnall/time-series-classification/src/f4fe66b74e039b0475a87ebf6d57db400da25b63/TimeSeriesClassification/src/tsc_algorithms/COTE.java?at=default&fileviewer=file-view-default

They're very up front about the terrible time complexity of their current implementations. But I think people could probably improve upon that if re-implementing for speed. 

This is certainly not an immediate or even near-term project to take on. And I assume that you've already got some of these transforms baked into tsfresh. But I'm interested in trying out some of these techniques in my own work, and figured you guys would probably be interested in some of this stuff too.

issue_title: activate script breaks prompt on 4.4.0-4.4.4
issue_body:
I have the following bash prompt:
```
$ echo $PS1
\u@\h:\w$(chg prompt '[\[\e[0;32m\]{\[\e[0;31m\]{status|modified}}{branch}\[\e[0m\]{{update}}] ' 2>/dev/null)$(__git_ps1)$
```
Up to version 4.3.27, conda works fine with that. Starting from 4.4.0, it breaks the prompt when I source the `activate` script:
```
$ echo $PS1
(dask36) \u@\h:\w$(chg prompt [[e[0
```

issue_title: Tried to Install scikit-learn with conda, conda and Python modules removed
issue_body: Hi, I just tried to use conda to install scikit-learn on my Mac OS X Yosemite machine, and the install failed at the linking stage.  After the failure, I tried to run the install again, and the conda executable was nowhere to be found, as well as typical python modules (Numpy, etc) when I ran python directly.  I am reinstalling Anaconda now, which will replace the python modules, but I can't give any info from conda because it is gone!

**I'm submitting a...** - [ ] bug report - [ ] feature request ## Current Behavior ### Steps to Reproduce ``` $conda install theano Solving environment: done ## Package Plan ## environment location: /Users/aaronmeurer/anaconda3 added / updated specs: - theano The following packages will be downloaded: package | build ---------------------------|----------------- pygpu-0.7.6 | py35_0 681 KB conda-forge theano-1.0.2 | py35_0 3.7 MB conda-forge libgpuarray-0.7.6 | 0 195 KB conda-forge ------------------------------------------------------------ Total: 4.6 MB The following NEW packages will be INSTALLED: libgpuarray: 0.7.6-0 conda-forge pygpu: 0.7.6-py35_0 conda-forge theano: 1.0.2-py35_0 conda-forge Downloading and Extracting Packages pygpu-0.7.6 | 681 KB | ######################################################################################## | 75% theano-1.0.2 | 3.7 MB | #######################################################################################8 | 75% libgpuarray-0.7.6 | 195 KB | ########################################################################################9 | 76% ValueError('n (-0.002934394253603312) cannot be negative',) ValueError('n (-0.0005246194491226319) cannot be negative',) ValueError('n (-0.01026341512318607) cannot be negative',) ``` If I then run `conda install theano` again it succeeds without redownloading, so I guess the download actually worked, but it caused conda to fail. I've already seen this twice since updating to conda 4.5.4. ## Expected Behavior ## Environment Information

issue_title: Always load all variants for MT as default
issue_body:
1000G file: all_wgs_-phase3_v5b.2013-05-02, do not contain MT frequencies and hence MT variants are not filtered from the analysis even if they are common. But they are lowered on frequency in the rank score modell using Mitomap frequencies. 

From a clinical perspective it is desirable to always load all MT variants into Scout i.e. disregard the deafult rank score cut-off when loading. From a WGS MT variant are usually less than 50, so this will not burden the database.

issue_title: Floating point exception
issue_body:
Perhaps a somewhat silly edge case, but probably nice to have this wrapped with a friendly python exception.  Observed on OSX + Anaconda + bcolz 0.10

```
import bcolz
x = bcolz.carray(zeros((0, 0), dtype='int'))
~/opt/bin/python.app: line 3: 85964 Floating point exception: 8   ~/opt/python.app/Contents/MacOS/python "$@"
```

issue_title: Close with delete aliases does not correctly close non alaised indices
issue_body:
Suppose you have three indices 
1. foo
2. bar
3. baz

that match a Curator filter of some kind and are actionable under the close action.

Suppose as well you have one alias `bang` that points at indices `foo` and `bar`.

Suppose you perform a `close` action on these indices. Indices `foo` and `bar` will be closed; `baz` will not, because it doesn't have an alias.

This seems counter intuitive to me. It seems like Curator should delete an alias if it exists with this option, but continue with regularly scheduled programming if one does not.

issue_title: Azure CLI Feature: Make aliases for common commands like "create"
issue_body:
### Environment summary

**Install Method:** How did you install the CLI? (e.g. pip, interactive script, apt-get, Docker, MSI, nightly)  
Answer here: pip

**CLI Version:** What version of the CLI and modules are installed? (Use `az --version`)  
Answer here: azure-cli (2.0.3)

**OS Version:** What OS and version are you using?  
Answer here: macOS 10.12.3

**Shell Type:** What shell are you using? (e.g. bash, cmd.exe, Bash on Windows)  
Answer here: bash on macOS

---

### Description

We often need to `create` a resource using the CLI. Aliases are super common with many CLI's. Some of our Azure CLI flags have aliases, but our commands should benefit from this too. Consider how often we create something ... we currently type:

`az group create -n PapaResourceGroup -l "East US"`

but we could type

`az group c -n PapaResourceGroup -l "East US"`

Now consider 3 commands in a row ...

```
az group create -n PapaResourceGroup -l eastus

az storage account create -n papastorage -g PapaResourceGroup --sku Standard_LRS -l eastus

az functionapp create -n papafunction -g PapaResourceGroup -s papastorage -c eastus
``

now see this ...

```
az group c -n PapaResourceGroup -l eastus

az storage account c -n papastorage -g PapaResourceGroup --sku Standard_LRS -l eastus

az functionapp c -n papafunction -g PapaResourceGroup -s papastorage -c eastus
``

I also think it would be great to have aliases for other commands, but the quick win would be "create" since we use it in so many places.

cc // @SyntaxC4

issue_title: Extension "webapp" commmand "remote-connection" not working on Win8.1 powershell 4
issue_body:
When trying to use the Azure CLI to create an tcp tunnel for a remote SSH connection to a Web app on linux, using the command "az webapp remote-connection create -n <webapp name> -g <resource group name>", The command throws a 503 error and hangs.  

**To Reproduce**

1. On Win8.1 install Azure CLI via the MSI at: https://aka.ms/installazurecliwindows
2. Open Powershell
3. Install the webapp extension with "az extension add --name webapp"
4. Login with "az login" go to URL in powershell message (https://microsoft.com/devicelogin) and enter the provided code, and follow the prompts, successfully log in.
5. List accounts with "az account list"
6. Choose correct account with "az account set --subscription <account id>"
7. List webapps in account with "az webapp list"
8. Attempt to create remote connection to app with "az webapp remote-connection create --name <webapp name> --resource-group <resource group name>
9. It picks a random port, then throws a 503 and hangs.

Auto-selecting port: 29547
Exception in thread Thread-1:
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "threading.py", line 916, in _bootstrap_inner
  File "threading.py", line 864, in run
  File "C:\Users\<my user>\.azure\cliextensions\webapp\azext_webapp\custom.py",
line 270, in _start_tunnel
    if not _check_for_ready_tunnel(remote_debugging_enabled, tunnel_server):
  File "C:\Users\<my user>\.azure\cliextensions\webapp\azext_webapp\custom.py",
line 240, in _check_for_ready_tunnel
    default_port = tunnel_server.is_port_set_to_default()
  File "C:\Users\<my user>\.azure\cliextensions\webapp\azext_webapp\tunnel.py",
line 95, in is_port_set_to_default
    raise CLIError("Failed to connect to '{}' with status code '{}' and reason '
{}'".format(url, r.status, r.reason))
knack.util.CLIError: Failed to connect to 'https://<webapp name>.scm.azurew
ebsites.net/AppServiceTunnel/Tunnel.ashx?GetStatus' with status code '503' and r
eason 'Service Unavailable'

No tunnel is created.  Have to CTRL+C to terminate command and return to prompt.

**Expected behavior**
Command should create a tunnel to the webapp allowing SSH access, according to documentation.

**Environment summary**
Install Method: 
1. install Azure CLI via the MSI at: https://aka.ms/installazurecliwindows
2. Open Powershell
3. Install the webapp extension with "az extension add --name webapp" 
      
CLI version: 2.0.33
webapp extension version: 0.2.6
OS version: Windows 8.1 Pro
Shell Type: Powershell 4.0 
same result in cmd.exe

**Additional context**
Doesn't matter whether you set a default resource group or declare the resource group name as part of the command.
Doesn't matter whether you declare the port number or let it select it automatically.
Doesn't matter which slot you choose, although on the default "production" slot it throws a different error:  "The Resource 'Microsoft.Web/sites/<webapp name>/slots/production' under resource group '<resource-group name>' was not found.  If you create another slot copied from production and try to connect to that, it throws the 503 error.   Doesn't matter which slot is active.

issue_title: "az mysql server create --name olivermysqlserver" allocates a DNS entry olivermysqlserver.mysql.database.azure.com. and will lead to name collision with other Azure clients, therefore az create will fail for common names like sqlserver
issue_body:
when creating a SaaS MySQL Server or SQL Server a public DNS entry for the domain mysql.database.azure.com will be allocated. That DNS entry must be unique, therefore common names like sqlserver will clash and the creation will fail.

reproduce:
az mysql server create --resource-group myResourceGroup --name olivermysqlserver  --location westeurope --admin-user myadmin  \
--admin-password nimda0123QWERTUIOP --sku-name GP_Gen4_2 --version 5.7

For each MySQL Server name a public DNS Entry will be created, e.g. olivermysqlserver.mysql.database.azure.com. The creation of the SaaS Service will fail, if the name is already occupied

issue_title: Feedback needed: library split!
issue_body:
Dear community,

We are currently working on splitting the library into different services, thus 4 new packages:
- azure-storage-blob
- azure-storage-file
- azure-storage-queue
- azure-storage-table

The split allows users to install only the service that they need. Anything that was previously directly under `azure.storage` are now in the new package `azure-storage-common`, which is a requirement for the new packages mentioned above. For example, `azure.storage.CloudStorageAccount` becomes `azure.storage.common.CloudStorageAccount`. 

The change is sitting on this [branch](https://github.com/Azure/azure-storage-python/tree/split-library), please take a look if you are interested. Any feedback is welcome! 😄  **Note: the new packages are not published to pypi yet.**

You can do `python3 build_packages.py all` to have all the packages built and put under the directory ./dist. Please follow the readme for more detailed instructions.

issue_body:
## Long story short

I'm connecting to an IOT device (airconditioner) with a REST interface.

Posting commands using >=3.0 doesn't work. The post goes through and the status OK is returned, but there's some issue with the TCP connection. 

## Steps to reproduce

Within the [wireshark packet capture](https://github.com/aio-libs/aiohttp/files/2306190/AIO-Issue.zip) there's two different TCP transactions with exactly the same aiohttp code running (also within the zip file). The short version of the code without imports etc looks like this:

```python
device_ip = '10.0.0.107'

async def send_command_async(command: str, data: Any):
    body = {command : data}
    url = f"http://{device_ip}/{command}"
    async with ClientSession() as session:
        async with session.post(url, json=body) as response:
            response.raise_for_status()

loop: AbstractEventLoop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
loop.run_until_complete(loop.create_task(send_command_async('SystemMODE', 'heat')))
```

If you get aiohttp<3.0 you get the first packet capture, up until packet 10. Everything is happy.

With the latest version and any version above 3.0, all goes well until the ACK gets sent for the OK packet, then a whole spume of out of order and dup ack packets goes back and forth.

I can't actually see any difference in what gets sent or anything. It's possible it's the device that's the issue, I've emailed the guys that produce the device so they are on this issue too, but the low level TCP stuff is a bit beyond me.

The odd thing is that http GET works just fine.

## Environment

* Python 3.6
* Windows 10
* Latest aiohttp (but did a regression test back until <3.0 version)
* The device is an [iZone air conditioner](http://izone.com.au/)

issue_title: Python MultiDict constructor discards generator content
issue_body:
When using the pure Python implementation, if a `MultiDict` is constructed with a generator argument:

    headers = CIMultiDict(
        (
            k.decode('utf-8', 'surrogateescape'),
            v.decode('utf-8', 'surrogateescape'),
        )
        for k, v in event.headers
    )

then the resulting `MultiDict` will be empty, instead of containing the key/value pairs as expected. This is because the generator is iterated over twice, and the first iteration discards all of the pairs.

issue_title: Late submission penalty
issue_body:
There is a bug with not resetting the late submission penalty if the due-date or timestamp changed such that the submission is no longer late.

To re-create (using the "zero" late submission plugin):
- Set the due-date and timestamp such that the submission is late
- Autograde
- Change the due-date or timestamp such that the submission is no longer late
- Autograde again (--force)
- Feedback will still show a late submission penalty

I have a fix for this, but this issue serves as a reminder to push it through.

issue_title: Nginx Jupyter notebook with sub location doesn't work
Stuck at 'Kernel starting, please wait...'
issue_body:
Hi,

I have the issue that my notebooks do not connect to their kernel anymore. I can create new notebooks and open old ones, but the white "Kernel starting, please wait..." box never goes away and cells never execute.

Where should I look to find out, what could be the cause for that?

The setup is: 
- The jupyter notebooks are running on a ubuntu server in the local lan and 
- I try to access them via my work notebook using Chrome 50.0.2661.86 (64-bit) on OSX 10.11.4 (15E65). 
- I access the notebooks using https with a password.
- My colleagues with similar configurations have no issues.

issue_title: Check if a file called in __manifest__.py exists in the filesystem
issue_body:
Hello, I recently had an error while building a custom module:

![17132811017-skortkefvw jpg](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/442938/25256970/aaccfe1e-2602-11e7-98af-b19f17ecd2bf.png)

This error was caused because I deleted the unnecessary file from module's folder, but I forgot to remove it from the `__manifest__.py` file.

Could be possible `pylint-odoo` make that check to `data` and `demo` elements declared into modules manifest, to avoid this kind of errors?

issue_title: Failed to import yara module.

issue_title: Subclassing models with REGEX validation crashes Python
issue_body:
If you add a pattern to the `regex=` argument for `StringType()` when you subclass the module Python will error with:

```
TypeError: Error when calling the metaclass bases
    cannot deepcopy this pattern object
```

Since `validate_regex()` isn't called in a tight loop and Python internally caches patterns I think just storing the string instead of the compiled pattern is a smart way to go.

This change will enable deepcopy to work and enable subclassing of models with `StringType(regex=...)` to work. Also the feels a little like premature optimization anyway.

PR #321

issue_title: Only doing assert_command fails linting
shed_upload silent if missing .shed.yml include files
issue_body:
My reading of the code suggests that the default ``include`` value is ``**`` (all files next to the ``.shed.yml`` file). I have tried adding an explicit listing of files, e.g.

```
$ more ~/repositories/pico_galaxy/tools/venn_list/.shed.yml
name: venn_list
owner: peterjc
homepage_url: https://github.com/peterjc/pico_galaxy/tools/venn_list
remote_repository_url: https://github.com/peterjc/pico_galaxy/tools/venn_list
description: Draw Venn Diagram (PDF) from lists, FASTA files, etc
long_description: |
  Draw Venn Diagrams for 1, 2 or 3 sets of identifiers as a PDF file.

  Can parse FASTA, FASTQ, SFF or tabular files (taking column one) for identifiers.
  This can be combined with the extensive tabular file filtering and manipulation
  tools within Galaxy to prepare the input data.

  Uses the R/Bioconductor package limma to draw the PDF file, called from Python with
  rpy.

  Uses Biopython to parse SFF files.
categories:
- Graphics
- Sequence Analysis
- Visualization
type: unrestricted
include:
- README.rst
- venn_list_with_typo.py
- venn_list.xml
- tool_dependencies.xml
```

This fails to complain about missing ``venn_list_with_typo.py``:

```
$ planemo shed_upload --tar_only  ~/repositories/pico_galaxy/tools/venn_list 
cp /tmp/tmpx0zfjB shed_upload.tar.gz
$ tar -tzf /tmp/tmpx0zfjB
tool_dependencies.xml
README.rst
venn_list.xml
```

On the positive side, it did not include ``venn_list.py`` because it wasn't asked to (deliberate mismatch with ``venn_list_with_typo.py``).

I want this example to fail, complaining that ``venn_list_with_typo.py`` was listed in the includes, but not found. Ideally the error would list all named but missing files, but just the first missing file would be acceptable.

As an aside, would checking the include files exist also fall under ``planemo lint`` functionality for validating the ``.shed.yml``?

issue_title: listen_forever always waits the maximum amount of time between sync retries
issue_body:
See this line: https://github.com/matrix-org/matrix-python-sdk/blob/master/matrix_client/client.py#L375 which sets the value for `bad_sync_timeout` to 5000 _seconds_ (probably intended to be milliseconds?), and this line: https://github.com/matrix-org/matrix-python-sdk/blob/master/matrix_client/client.py#L386 which calls `time.sleep` with that value. Note that `time.sleep` expects seconds, not millis.

This means that if `_sync` fails with an HTTP 500, the client will always wait `self.bad_sync_timeout_max` seconds (currently 1 hour) before retrying.

It looks like there's supposed to be some exponential backoff logic between retries, but because the initial timeout is set so high it always selects the ceiling of one hour.

If you look at the hs37d5 BAMs, there are a fair number of hard clipped reads (but these all seem to be supplemental alignments). I'm going to add this to the pipeline (it's baked into BWAKIT, IIRC, so it's a simple add) and I think we should run a sample through with the adapter trimming just to sanity check.

issue_title: Visualization Tutorial: too many arguments for ModularServer?
issue_body:
I tried to follow visualization tutorial but the following line did not work:
```
server = ModularServer(MoneyModel,
                       [grid],
                       "Money Model",
                       100, 10, 10)
```
I get the following error:
`TypeError: __init__() takes from 3 to 5 positional arguments but 7 were given`
However, it goes away, if I turn the last three arguments into an dictionary:
```
 server = ModularServer(MoneyModel,
                        [grid],
                        "Money Model",
                        {"N": 100,
                         "width": 10,
                         "height": 10})
```

Then the visualization runs without problems.

issue_title: `addopts = --failed-first` causes "Different tests were collected"  error
issue_body:
I suppose cache for each gateway is separate, that's why different gateways report tests in different order. 
The `--failed-first` setting should be ignored when reporting tests to the master node.

issue_title: iControlRESTSession needs to support the token=true argument for token auth
issue_body:
Starting with version 12.0 of TMOS token based authentication and authorization is supported by iControl REST.

BIG-IP remote-users access via iControl REST requires token based authorization. f5-common-python access to BIG-IP needs to support token authorization when access BIG-IP devices which are configured for remote-user support. This is a base requirement for all MSPs and most enterprises.

Use of token based authorization needs to be an option parameter for the connection as support for TMOS versions prior to 12.0 is also a requirement.

My code has a list of classes, and at the end of every epoch its supposed to go through the list and log scalars, tagging them "recall_classname", "precision_classname" and so forth. 

What's showing up in tensorboard isn't those names though, its "recall_", "recall_/1", "recall_/2", and so forth, then finally "recall_.", etc. 

What am I doing wrong?

The code is:

```
classestouse = [2,3,4,9,12,13,16,17]
classes = ["none", "comma", "period", "colon", "questionmark", "semicolon", "and", "exclamationpoint"]

def on_end_epoch(state):
    logger.log_value("loss_mean", avgmeter.value()[1], state['epoch'])
    logger.log_value("loss_sd", avgmeter.value()[1], state['epoch'])
    avgacc = sum(classmeter.value()) / len(classmeter.value())
    logger.log_value("loss_avgacc", avgacc, state['epoch'])
    state['iterator'].set_postfix(accuracy = avgacc, loss = avgmeter.value()[0], losssd = avgmeter.value()[1])
    confusion = confusionmeter.value()
    if state['train']:
        for i, v in zip(classestouse, classes):
            correct = confusion[i][i]
            groundtruth = confusion[i].sum()
            predicted = confusion[:, i].sum()
            recall = correct / groundtruth
            precision = correct / predicted
            v = punctypes.vocab.itos[i]
            logger.log_value("recall_%s" % (v), recall, state['epoch'])
            logger.log_value("precision_%s" % (v), precision, state['epoch'])
            logger.log_value('accuracy_%s' % (v), classmeter.value(i), state['epoch'])
```

issue_title: bytes/str conversion lines not hit in test_isoparser
issue_body:
I've noticed that in the [coverage for `test_isoparser`](https://codecov.io/gh/dateutil/dateutil/src/572e358279fb0d90862e2f5fd8d55300a9670fcf/dateutil/test/test_isoparser.py), lines [375](https://github.com/dateutil/dateutil/blob/a41c4c6b498189b1c456e2d8e7a21a308974bc89/dateutil/test/test_isoparser.py#L375), [447](https://github.com/dateutil/dateutil/blob/a41c4c6b498189b1c456e2d8e7a21a308974bc89/dateutil/test/test_isoparser.py#L447) and [449]([447](https://github.com/dateutil/dateutil/blob/a41c4c6b498189b1c456e2d8e7a21a308974bc89/dateutil/test/test_isoparser.py#L449)) are not being hit. I can't tell if this means that the tests aren't being run against both `bytes` and `str` or if it means that I have unnecessary `if` conditions in the tests.

If the former, we need to make sure tests are being run against `bytes` and `str`. If the latter, we can remove the unnecessary branches.

The parts that are not covered because they are under an `xfail` can have `#pragma: no cover` added to them, as can the error handling lines in the test generation part.

issue_title: Add integration test.
Pull the urllib3-native exception out, and raise it
Double quote symbol leads to escaping when serializing errors to json
issue_body:
Hi there!

Thanks for the work you've done on the library. I met a pretty minor issue that I want to report: when you generate a validation error for the email field you put a double quote there which then leads to unnecessary escaping when you try to send this validation back to client. Solution is pretty easy — wrap parameter value with single quote not double quote. It still maintains readability, but don't conflict with JSON symbols anymore. I'm attaching code below to showcase what I mean.

Thanks in advance!

    In [1]: from marshmallow import fields, Schema

    In [2]: from simplejson import dumps

    In [3]: class Test(Schema):
        ...:         email = fields.Email()
        ...:     

    In [4]: schema = Test()

    In [5]: _, errors = schema.load({'email': 'example@example.c'})

    In [6]: errors
    Out[6]: {'email': [u'"example@example.c" is not a valid email address.']}

    In [7]: dumps(errors)
    Out[7]: '{"email": ["\\"example@example.c\\" is not a valid email address."]}'

issue_title: marshmallow-jsonapi forcing loading of related data
issue_body:
I'm using marshmallow-jsonapi with sqlalchemy, and it's forcing loading of related data, when all  I want is for marshmallow-json to create a link to related data. Here's a snippet of what I'm doing:

```
class Server(db.Model):
    __tablename__ = 'servers'

    id = db.Column(db.Integer,
                   primary_key=True)
    hostname = db.Column(db.String(255, collation='utf8_bin'),
                         unique=True,
                         nullable=False)

    jobs = db.relationship('Job', back_populates='server')


class Job(db.Model):
    __tablename__ = 'jobs'

    id = db.Column(db.Integer,
                   primary_key=True)
    server_id = db.Column(db.Integer, db.ForeignKey('servers.id'),
                          nullable=False)
    name = db.Column(db.String(255, collation='utf8_bin'),
                     index=True,
                     nullable=False)

    server = db.relationship('Server', back_populates='jobs')


class JobSchema(Schema):
    id = fields.Integer(dump_only=True)
    name = fields.String()

    server = Relationship(
        related_view='.server_detail',
        related_view_kwargs={'server_id': '<server_id>'},
        include_data=False,
    )

    class Meta:
        type_ = 'jobs'

data = JobSchema().dump(Job.query.all(), many=True).data
```

Rendering these jobs triggers an access to a job's `server` field, which triggers a SQL query to fetch the server, even though the fetched server record is never used. It would be great if marshmallow-jsonapi was able to suppress marshmallow accessing this field.

As a workaround, I can cheat the relationship, and just add a `attribute="id"` to the `Relationship` constructor, which I know will always be present on the record. It's not ideal, but it works.

issue_title: Early data missed on open on Posix systems
issue_body: serialposix.Serial.open calls self.reset_input_buffer() after opening which causes the underlying data that's been buffering to be dropped.  FWIW serialwin32.Serial.open does not call this function on open.

issue_title: DenseMatrix functionality improvements
issue_body:
After a slight brush with DenseMatrix, here's some stuff I found not working that well:
DenseMatrix is missing \_\_sub\_\_, currently can do `A+B`, but not `A-B`.
DenseMatrix does not support left side multiple with a Basic, that is `Symbol('x')*A` fails. Add \_\_rmul\_\_ or modify Basic?
Adding and multiplying of DenseMatrix objects of incompatible size should result in Python errors/exceptions, not crashes.
Indexing of a single column or row matrix should work with a single index, like `A[1]`.
Add vector specific methods like dot, norm, cross (I suppose this should be done on the symengine/C++ side actually first).

issue_title: Inconsistency in string theory
issue_body:
Feeding z3 (compiled from commit 71ca355 from late November, I'm about to try a newer version to see if it goes away) the input
```
(declare-const x String)

(assert (not (str.prefixof "prefix:" x)))

(assert (or
         (str.prefixof "prefix:a" x)
         (str.prefixof "prefix:b" x)
         (str.prefixof "prefix:c" x)))
(check-sat)
(get-model)
```
yields 
```
sat
(model 
  (define-fun x () String
    "prefix:b")
)
```
which clearly doesn't satisfy the first assertion  `(assert (not (str.prefixof "prefix:" x)))`.

This goes away (i.e. z3 says "unsat" as expected) if I shorten "prefix:" to just "x" or have fewer than three alternatives in the disjunction
```
 (or
         (str.prefixof "prefix:a" x)
         (str.prefixof "prefix:b" x)
         (str.prefixof "prefix:c" x))
```
if that helps debugging.

                      (union (dom@@Pcs@@State f1) (dom@@Pcs@@State f2)))
                   :pattern

                   :pattern
                   ( (elem@Open@@pfun@@Pcs@@State@Close empty-fun@@Pcs@@State
                                                        (tfun@@Pcs@@State empty-set@@Pcs s2)) ))))
(assert (forall ( (f1 (pfun Pcs State))
                  (s1 (set Pcs))
                  (x Pcs) )
                (! (=> (elem@@Pcs x (set-diff@@Pcs (dom@@Pcs@@State f1) s1))
                       (= (apply@@Pcs@@State (dom-subt@@Pcs@@State s1 f1) x)
                          (apply@@Pcs@@State f1 x)))
                   :pattern
                   ( (apply@@Pcs@@State (dom-subt@@Pcs@@State s1 f1) x) ))))

                  (s1 (set Pcs))
                  (x Pcs) )

                            (elem@@Pcs x (dom@@Pcs@@State f1)))
                       (= (apply@@Pcs@@State (dom-rest@@Pcs@@State s1 f1) x)
                          (apply@@Pcs@@State f1 x)))
                   :pattern
                   ( (apply@@Pcs@@State (dom-rest@@Pcs@@State s1 f1) x) ))))
(assert (forall ( (x Pcs)
                  (y State) )
                (! (= (apply@@Pcs@@State (mk-fun@@Pcs@@State x y) x) y)
                   :pattern
                   ( (apply@@Pcs@@State (mk-fun@@Pcs@@State x y) x) ))))
(assert (forall ( (f1 (pfun Pcs State))
                  (f2 (pfun Pcs State))
                  (x Pcs) )
                (! (=> (and (elem@@Pcs x (dom@@Pcs@@State f1))
                            (not (elem@@Pcs x (dom@@Pcs@@State f2))))
                       (= (apply@@Pcs@@State (ovl@@Pcs@@State f1 f2) x)
                          (apply@@Pcs@@State f1 x)))
                   :pattern
                   ( (apply@@Pcs@@State (ovl@@Pcs@@State f1 f2) x) ))))
(assert (forall ( (f1 (pfun Pcs State))
                  (f2 (pfun Pcs State))
                  (x Pcs) )
                (! (=> (elem@@Pcs x (dom@@Pcs@@State f2))
                       (= (apply@@Pcs@@State (ovl@@Pcs@@State f1 f2) x)
                          (apply@@Pcs@@State f2 x)))
                   :pattern
                   ( (apply@@Pcs@@State (ovl@@Pcs@@State f1 f2) x) ))))
(assert (forall ( (x Pcs)
                  (y State) )
                (! (= (dom@@Pcs@@State (mk-fun@@Pcs@@State x y))
                      (mk-set@@Pcs x))
                   :pattern
                   ( (dom@@Pcs@@State (mk-fun@@Pcs@@State x y)) ))))
(assert (forall ( (f1 (pfun Pcs State)) )
                (! (= (ovl@@Pcs@@State empty-fun@@Pcs@@State f1) f1)
                   :pattern
                   ( (ovl@@Pcs@@State empty-fun@@Pcs@@State f1) ))))
(assert (forall ( (f1 (pfun Pcs State)) )
                (! (= (ovl@@Pcs@@State f1 empty-fun@@Pcs@@State) f1)
                   :pattern
                   ( (ovl@@Pcs@@State f1 empty-fun@@Pcs@@State) ))))
(assert (= (dom@@Pcs@@State empty-fun@@Pcs@@State)
           empty-set@@Pcs))
(assert (forall ( (x Pcs) )
                (! (= (select ident@@Pcs x) x)
                   :pattern
                   ( (select ident@@Pcs x) ))))
(assert (forall ( (x Pcs)
                  (y Pcs) )
                (! (= (select (const@@Pcs@@Pcs x) y) x)
                   :pattern
                   ( (select (const@@Pcs@@Pcs x) y) ))))
; SKIP:d
(assert (= d@prime d))

                  (p_1 Pcs) )
                (! (= (elem@Open@@Pair@@Pcs@@Pcs@Close (pair p_0 p_1) net)
                      (elem@Open@@Pair@@Pcs@@Pcs@Close (pair p_1 p_0) net))
                   :pattern

; m0:act0

            (forall ( (p Pcs) )
                    (=> true (= (apply@@Pcs@@State st p) idle)))))

                     (set@@Pcs@@Pcs (@@lambda@@_0 st@prime idle) ident@@Pcs))))

                (set@@Pcs@@Pcs (@@lambda@@_0 st idle) ident@@Pcs)))

issue_title: Cannot understand behavior of Z3 with unsat-core
issue_body:
The title is the same of my post on StackOverflow:
["cannot-understand-behavior-of-z3-with-unsat-core" on Stackoverflow](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/51380179/cannot-understand-behavior-of-z3-with-unsat-core)

A user suggested me to open a ticket here, so here I am.
_I apologize if I'm wasting your time but an answer on this problem would be very important for my master's thesis._

I know that Z3 cannot guarantee a minimal unsat-core response, but what I see in the output of the next script, is very strange for me.

In the script I try to check simple expression like this (in some pseudo-language):

`(n_number == 22) AND (n_text == "abcd") AND (n_number == 44)`

Clearly the result is unsat, caused by first and third clause.

The strange things (for me) is that unsat-core response changes based on the order of my (named) asserts.

I think that the second clause could never be in the unsat response, but in the case 4 I see it. 

**RESULT for my script "little3pieces"**
```
---------- CASE 3: assert on all pieces (order: 1,2,3) 
unsat
(as22val as22noErr as44val as44noMiss namedP1t namedP3t) 

---------- CASE 4: assert on all pieces (order: 1,3,2)
unsat
(as22val as44val namedP1t namedP2t namedP3t)

```
_**In the last case namedP2t appears!!!**_

Anyone could give me an explanation?

In my script each type has 2 flags, so I have:
- TValue Int --> an Int with a bool for ERRor and a bool for MISSing
- TValue Bool --> a Bool with a bool for ERRor and a bool for MISSing

and so on...)


** ----- My Script ----- ** 
```
(set-option :produce-unsat-cores true)
; REMEMBER: set name on assert 

(set-option :interactive-mode true)  ;;; <<< TO USE (get-assertions)


;#######################
;RECORD DATATYPE with BOTTOM and ERROR
;#######################
(declare-datatypes (U) ((TValue (mk-val (val U)(miss Bool)(err Bool)))))


;################### MyString DECLARATIONs #######################
 (declare-datatypes () ((MyString s_abcd  s_AdditionalStringValue )))

;################### FACPL FUNCTION DECLARATIONs #######################

(define-fun isTrue ((x (TValue Bool))) Bool
    (ite (= x (mk-val true false false)) true false)
)


(define-fun equalInt ((x (TValue Int)) (y (TValue Int))) (TValue Bool)
    (ite (or (err x) (err y))
        (mk-val false false true)
        (ite (or (miss x) (miss y))
            (mk-val false true false)
            (ite (= (val x) (val y))
                (mk-val true false false)
                (mk-val false false false)
            )
        )
    )
)

;; SIMILAR VERSION
(define-fun equalString ((x (TValue MyString)) (y (TValue MyString))) (TValue Bool)
    (ite (or (err x) (err y))
        (mk-val false false true)
        (ite (or (miss x) (miss y))
            (mk-val false true false)
            (ite (= (val x) (val y))
                (mk-val true false false)
                (mk-val false false false)
            )
        )
    )
)

;########### END DATATYPEs AND FUNCTIONs DECLARATION #######



;############# ATTRIBUTE DECLARATIONs ###################
(declare-const n_number (TValue Int))
;; ORIG without name... (assert (not (and (miss n_number) (err n_number))))
(assert (! (not (and (miss n_number) (err n_number))) :named asNumberConstraint ))


(declare-const n_text (TValue MyString))
;; ORIG without name... (assert (not (and (miss n_text) (err n_text))))
(assert (! (not (and (miss n_text) (err n_text))) :named asTextConstraint ))


;########### CONSTANTs DECLARATIONs ##################

;;; ------------------------------------- CONSTANT INT = 22
(declare-const const_22 (TValue Int))

; ORIG without name
;(assert (= (val const_22) 22))
;(assert (not (miss const_22))) 
;(assert (not (err const_22)))

; same with name 
(assert (! (= (val const_22) 22) :named as22val) )
(assert (! (not (miss const_22)) :named as22noMiss) )
(assert (! (not (err const_22))  :named as22noErr) )



;;; ------------------------------------- CONSTANT INT = 44
(declare-const const_44 (TValue Int))

; ORIG without name
;(assert (= (val const_44) 44))
;(assert (not (miss const_44))) 
;(assert (not (err const_44)))

(assert (! (= (val const_44) 44) :named as44val) )
(assert (! (not (miss const_44)) :named as44noMiss) )
(assert (! (not (err const_44))  :named as44noErr) )



;;; ------------------------------------- CONSTANT (MY)STRING = s_abcd
(declare-const const_abcd (TValue MyString))

; ORIG without name
;(assert (= (val const_abcd) s_abcd))
;(assert (not (miss const_abcd))) 
;(assert (not (err const_abcd)))

(assert (! (= (val const_abcd) s_abcd) :named asABCDval) )
(assert (! (not (miss const_abcd))     :named asABCDnoMiss) )
(assert (! (not (err const_abcd))      :named asABCDnoErr) )



;######## END ATTRIBUTEs AND CONSTANTs DECLARATION ###############


; --------- BEFORE THIS LINE ALL ASSERTS ARE GIVEN ---------
; --------- but they could be wrong anyway !!!!!!! ---------
; -- so, please, give me hints about all possible defects --



; ===============================================
; START VERIFICATION MADE WITH MY ASSERTS
; ===============================================


; I try to check a simple expression like this (in some pseudo-language):
; (n_number == 22) AND (n_text == "abcd") AND (n_number == 44)

(push)

(define-fun piece1 () (TValue Bool) (equalInt const_22 n_number))    ;;; piece1
(define-fun piece2 () (TValue Bool) (equalString const_abcd n_text)) ;;; piece2
(define-fun piece3 () (TValue Bool) (equalInt n_number const_44))    ;;; piece3


;;; (get-assertions)

; Clearly the result is unsat, caused by piece1 and piece3


; ---------- begin CASE 3 ----------
(push)
(echo "")
(echo "---------- CASE 3: assert on all pieces (order: 1,2,3)")

(assert (! (isTrue piece1) :named namedP1t))
(assert (! (isTrue piece2) :named namedP2t))  ; <<<< namedP2t in the middle
(assert (! (isTrue piece3) :named namedP3t))

(check-sat)
(get-unsat-core)

; I GET:
; 

(pop)
; ---------- end CASE 3 ----------




; NOW THE STRANGE THING!!!! (FOR ME)



; ---------- begin CASE 4 ----------
(push)
(echo "")
(echo "---------- CASE 4: assert on all pieces (order: 1,3,2)")

(assert (! (isTrue piece1) :named namedP1t))
(assert (! (isTrue piece3) :named namedP3t))
(assert (! (isTrue piece2) :named namedP2t))  ; <<<< namedP2t on bottom

(check-sat)
(get-unsat-core)

; I GET:
; (as22val as44val as44noErr namedP1t namedP2t namedP3t)
; NOW I SEE namedP2t !!!!! WHY?????????????????????????????????
; named2Pt is never the cause of unsat!!!!!!!

(pop)
; ---------- end CASE 4 ----------


```

The previous script is available at:
[https://rise4fun.com/Z3/Owcds](https://rise4fun.com/Z3/Owcds)

I left only cases 3 and 4 (both on result and script) to highlight the problem.


My example is already a simplification and I've kept "only the minimal things", but paying attention to not eliminate "the given system of asserts and functions". I suspect that the problem could be in this "given system" (**but I cannot understand why**) because trying with another example made with "normal" functions and operators using the "same" 3 clauses (**2 relevant and 1 irrelevant**), I get the correct result (a1,a3).
See example at: [rise4fun.com/Z3/plslPq](rise4fun.com/Z3/plslPq)

Sorry for the long post. I'm available for any clarification.
Thank you

issue_title: push & pop causes hang
issue_body:
Hi, I found the constraints cause hang if I use push & pop.
The constraints are 
```
(push)
(declare-fun k!0 () (_ BitVec 8))
(declare-fun k!1 () (_ BitVec 8))
(declare-fun k!2 () (_ BitVec 8))
(declare-fun k!3 () (_ BitVec 8))
(assert (let ((a!1 (concat ((_ extract 31 3)
                     (bvadd #x0000000b (concat k!3 k!2 k!1 k!0)))
                   #b000)))
(let ((a!2 (bvadd #x0804bfff
                  (concat ((_ extract 31 12) (bvadd #x0002100f a!1)) #x000)))
      (a!3 (bvadd #x0804b000
                  (concat ((_ extract 31 12) (bvadd #x0002100f a!1)) #x000))))
  (not (= (concat ((_ extract 31 12) a!2) #x000) a!3)))))
(check-sat)
(pop)
```

However, if I run
```
(declare-fun k!0 () (_ BitVec 8))
(declare-fun k!1 () (_ BitVec 8))
(declare-fun k!2 () (_ BitVec 8))
(declare-fun k!3 () (_ BitVec 8))
(assert (let ((a!1 (concat ((_ extract 31 3)
                     (bvadd #x0000000b (concat k!3 k!2 k!1 k!0)))
                   #b000)))
(let ((a!2 (bvadd #x0804bfff
                  (concat ((_ extract 31 12) (bvadd #x0002100f a!1)) #x000)))
      (a!3 (bvadd #x0804b000
                  (concat ((_ extract 31 12) (bvadd #x0002100f a!1)) #x000))))
  (not (= (concat ((_ extract 31 12) a!2) #x000) a!3)))))
(check-sat)
```
then, it simply returns `unsat`.

From my understanding of push & pop,
it saves contexts, so the first one should be same with the second one.
However, it causes hang. 
Could you let me know why they are different?

I am using the latest z3 wih 32bit version
```sh
$z3 --version
Z3 version 4.5.1 - 32 bit
```

Thanks.

```
restoring WeightLimits
OrderedDict([(u'id', u'4'), (u'source_doc', u'5'), (u'created', u'2016-04-05 00:07:29'), (u'modified', u'2016-04-05 00:07:29'), (u'by_user', u'legacy'), (u'units', u'us'), (u'max_takeoff_weight', u''), (u'max_fuel_load', u''), (u'max_payload', u''), (u'empty_weight', u''), (u'VFR_day_reserves', u''), (u'VFR_night_reserves', u''), (u'IFR_100nm_reserves', u'734'), (u'IFR_200nm_reserves', u'946'), (u'IFR_300nm_reserves', u'1132'), (u'NBAA_100nm_reserves', u'641'), (u'NBAA_200nm_reserves', u'851'), (u'NBAA_300nm_reserves', u'1035')]) could not convert string to float:

issue_title: Not clear how to use fudge.patch with pytest
issue_body:
I've started using **pytest** and want to continue using **fudge** rather than **mock** because I prefer the way fudge lets you declare expected calls and returns in once place.

However, when you `@fudge.patch` a test function, pytest interprets the function parameter as a fixture and raises an exception when it can't find one.

    @patch('some.other.thing')
    def test_something(fake_thing):
        fake_thing.expects('count').returns(123)
        result = something()
        assert result == 123

Running pytest raises:

    E       fixture 'fake_thing' not found

It's not clear from the fudge documentation how to use `fudge.patch` with pytest (search shows pytest is not mentioned anywhere).

issue_title: Make portable playlists
issue_body:
One problem that I face when making playlists on YouTube is that after spending time to make a playlist, sometimes the videos disappear (video made private, video deleted, etc.) without leaving behind any information about the deleted item.

I think this can be overcome by MPS by doing the following:
1. Add config options to save playlists to a desired location
2. Make playlists portable by writing them in yaml with enough information about the item, so that in case the original video is deleted, one at least has enough information about it to add a new source url. Also, syncing across computer will become feasible.

 def adjust_bars(bars, ex_factors, fields, adjust_type, adjust_orig):

issue_title: Accept wildcard for --inPfnListFile
issue_body:
It would be convenient to be able to wildcard expand the argument given to --inPfnListFile, e.g.

```ganga athena --local --inPfnListFile="./*" --split=12 --outputlocation="$HOME/ZdZd/ganga" ZdZdAnalysis/analysisJobOptions_r21.py```

where the current directory contains many text files, each corresponding to a dataset directory (e.g. `mc16_12345.txt` contains the absolute paths to the files `mc16_12345_1.root`, `mc16_12345_2.root`, etc... inside the directory `mc16_12345`).

Currently, this has to be done explicitly:

```ganga athena --local --inPfnListFile="mc16_12345.txt,mc16_67890.txt,etc..." --split=12 --outputlocation="$HOME/ZdZd/ganga" ZdZdAnalysis/analysisJobOptions_r21.py```

This is very cumbersome if I have to supply many text files (for instance, 15 text files corresponding to 15 different MC datasets and their folders).

I don't think this can be done with bash or zsh globbing either, since `*` expands to `mc16_12345.txt mc16_67890.txt` instead of `mc16_12345.txt,mc16_67890.txt` (which is the required argument format).

Thanks,
Justin

issue_title: kinto start takes too much time.
issue_body:
It can sometimes take more than 2 seconds, as reported by @n1k0 on JS clients integration tests.

I investigated a bit and found out, that on my machine, loading the entrypoint takes more than 1 second already:
```
$ time python -c "from pkg_resources import load_entry_point; load_entry_point('kinto', 'console_scripts', 'kinto')"
python -c   0,96s user 0,16s system 99% cpu 1,132 total
```

In comparison, `pserve` takes 200msec: 
```
$ time python -c "from pkg_resources import load_entry_point; load_entry_point('pyramid', 'console_scripts', 'pcreate')"
python -c   0,18s user 0,09s system 98% cpu 0,272 total
```

I realized that moving `import requests` from `cliquet.initialization` imports [PR](https://github.com/mozilla-services/cliquet/pull/674), and remove `import pip` from `kinto.__main__` I could reduce by half:
```
$ time python -c "from pkg_resources import load_entry_point; load_entry_point('kinto', 'console_scripts', 'kinto')"
python -c   0,36s user 0,18s system 98% cpu 0,543 total
```

I knew this was not going to speed up the `kinto start` command too much. I tracked down and noticed the `__main__:main` was executed twice because of `--reload` argument.

issue_title: MLE Announce processing corner case
issue_body:
Currently the Thread spec only specifies what to do if `local_timestamp > announce_timestamp` and what to do if `local_timestamp < announce_timestamp` The case where both timestamps are equal is unspecified.

OpenThread acts as if `local_timestamp > announce_timestamp` when both timestamps are equal. This causes a huge number of MLE Annouce Messages to be sent in rapid succession (see 
[InfiniteAnnounce.pcapng.gz](https://github.com/openthread/openthread/files/1138878/InfiniteAnnounce.pcapng.gz))

There is no behaviour specified in the Thread spec (raised the issue [SPEC-730](https://threadgroup.atlassian.net/browse/SPEC-730) to address this). I my opinion in this case it is not too bad to do nothing (no not apply the information from the MLE Announce and do not send any MLE announces yourself)

This would mean to split the `if ... else` into an `if ... elif ... else` construct in mle.cpp:3036. (I know `elif` does not exist in c++, but it explains it more clearly)
The above would address the issue in the short term (and is compliant with the spec), until the Thread group figures out what to do with the issue

issue_title: message buffer peak usage - verses "normality" & running out of buffers.
issue_body:
This is more of a question about openthreads message buffer implementation, and how buffer management is done.  It seems the default message buffer count is minimal at best and possibly far to small on the NCP device.

I have what I believe to be a simple test case -  NCP + Border Router  + One joining device - Android APP  - During the joining process, the peak buffer usage is 31 (of 40) buffers in use (75% are busy) leaving only 9 buffers, at 5 minutes into the process about 50% of the buffers are still busy - it takes a full 7 minutes to return to what I would call a normal level of activity (ie: 2 busy, 38 free)

it is important to note that in my example test network there is zero "application traffic" - it is only thread network self induced activity.

My question is along these lines - sadly it is not a distinct single question

* For the NCP - is the "40 buffers" reasonable?  Should this be larger?
* Or is this really small bare (spec) minimum, but not enough to be practical?

* If the NCP is a router, and in this example it is AND if there where other devices on the network (ie: sleep devices) and routing between devices - the other network traffic would there would be, and there is an amount of other traffic (there would be several) or other activity (ie: messages routed up stream to the public internet or to the local in-home network)) the NCP will probably run out of buffers during the commissioning process.

Below is some detail and background (data) to my question. 

-Duane.

COMMISSIONING TIME LINE
===================

At time 0 - the NCP is reset, and is not part of a network.

Within about 10 to 15 seconds - I have used the WEB browser and created a network, and I have used the Android App to scan a barcode to commission a new device.

IMPORTANT - Before hand - 

In the "message.cpp" file - where messages are allocated & freed, insert a bit of code to print out the total number of FREE messages each time a message is allocated or free. It is this number we are tracking from here on out.

In this simple use case - the result is a peak usage of 31 of the 40 (75%) leaving 9 free buffers.

If there is ANY other activity (ie:  The NCP might be a router that needs to forward messages) - it often runs out of messages.

Commissioning completes in about 1 minute 
Actual time in my test case is timestamp = 74 seconds.
But I'll use the 1 minute as a nice round number for this discussion.

I'm not - purposefully stopping the android app - I am being a "an average user who would not know to do this"

At +2 minutes (120 seconds)  - there are 18 to 19 free messages (22 are in use)
(there has been a little activity, a few messages allocated and freed)

At +3 minutes (180 seconds) - same: 18 to 19 free messages
(there has been a little activity, a few messages allocated and freed)

Note: at 234 seconds - the NCP announces "commissioner in active" caused by a system level commissioning timeout - I did not 'cancel' the process.

At +4 minutes (240 seconds) -  same: 18 to 19 free messages (20 free)

MOST of these messages are, and have been stuck/lingering in the coap response queue.

At +5 minutes (300 seconds) - the curve begins to bend... 22 free messages (50%)

At +6 minutes (360 seconds) - 31 free messages 
  20 seconds later - there are 32 free messages

At +7 minutes (420 seconds) - 37 free messages - (3 busy messages)

In my example network - a single sample mtd end device does not transmit, it emulates a binary 1/0 output (think lightbulb) - it can locally be turned on/off - or it can remotely (via  coap) change state. But to be clear: There is ZERO application layer network traffic. The numbers here are the network self induced message buffer usage.

From here on out - there are always 36 to 37 free messages - and I don't think there's much to say.

SIMPLE LINUX TEST APP - Polling Devices
===========================

Taking the completed above network (4 devices)
   2 of the devices are "paired" - a temperature sensor and a thermostat, they talk to each other
   2 of the devices are simple actuator type devices (button/light type) they do not talk to each other

On a linux box - I run a simple test script that does the following:

```
Loop Forever
     For device A, B, C, D
         using "libcoap example app: "coap-client" fetch data from the device.
         NOTE: this is "coap" (non-secure, not secure)

         Using "ping6" - ping the device.
     Sleep 5 seconds
```
Depending upon what is going on  - this peaks at about 10 buffers in use.  Meaning - commissioning has the possibility of having problems.  I do understand that UDP is lossy - and retries should occur...  but this example test case is small, it seems the this might be very "borderline" at best. 

Possible Bug?
===================

Lastly - I think there is a bug - this is why I started this investigation - but I'm not sure yet.

After running my simple forever loop test (described above) several hours (12 or so), to overnight (24hrs, or over the weekend) - the devices often loose (leak?) a message buffer

I think it would be helpful - on the posix simulation - to introduce some level of "lossy ness" to the network channel.  Meaning today - the network SEND operation is nearly perfect, for simulation purposes - I think it would be very helpful to add a bit of "random loss"  - where in the transmission process maybe  10 to 20% of the packets transmitted are thrown away and not transmitted (simulating a transmission jammer) and likewise in the simulated receiver, 10 to 20% of the packets are also thrown away  (simulating a receiver jammer)

Then - have some means to run the test process overnight for a very long time in both MTD and FTD modes.

-END-

issue_title: Seamlessly conversion nullableString (public inherited from std::string) to Python and back.

I'm have a class which inherits directly from the std::string class but also has the concept of 'null' versus empty (""). This is an important distinction because of it understanding "on the wire" IPC traffic which has different and important distinctions between null and "".

When a method is invoked that returns my class nullableString, Python complains with the following error:
what():  TypeError: Unable to convert function return value to a Python type! The signature was
    (self: Module.GetReply) -> nullableString

I've looked at the documentation and include custom type_casters but it's not clear how to use this to handle the conversion. I am looking at potentially using a custom type caster with pybind11, however, I'm hoping to find a simpler way since the only difference between my class and string is the concept of a null string. All regular methods (c_str(), begin(), end() etc.) are directly accessible to call.

I can add reproducible code, however, it is likely to be less succinct than this explanation.

Thanks,
Salah

issue_labels: [{'id': 210831837, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/area:%20cert%20management', 'name': 'area: cert management', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}, {'id': 200596079, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/area:%20ui%20/%20ux', 'name': 'area: ui / ux', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}, {'id': 281347824, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/has%20pr', 'name': 'has pr', 'color': '0052cc', 'default': False}, {'id': 493513299, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/potential%20workflow%20regression', 'name': 'potential workflow regression', 'color': 'c95054', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
## My operating system is (include version):
Xubuntu 17.10

## I installed Certbot with (certbot-auto, OS package manager, pip, etc):
git + certbot-auto

## I ran this command and it produced this output:
certbot-auto revoke -n --cert-path /path --key-path /path --reason cessationofoperation

## Certbot's behavior differed from what I expected because:
There is no option to operate non-interactively AND not delete the certificates. I was able to achieve this prior to https://github.com/certbot/issues/4914 and just got around to updating my scripting to discover this appears to no longer be possible.

This is a request for a flag to NOT delete certs when revoking non-interactively. The reason I need this is so the certificates can still be used to check OCSP and verify that they were successfully revoked, all via non-interactive automation.

issue_title: Add the instruction of the installation for Octave kernel at the SoS homepage
Frequency of checking job status.
issue_body:
Checking status of a job is not very easy, basically we need to check if a status file has been updated in the past 5 seconds or so for running jobs, and check the time stamp of task, result, and status files in other cases. A bigger problem is how frequently we should check the status of jobs.

We are checking the status of a job every 2 seconds at the beginning, and at about 30 seconds for long jobs. This does not sound like much but if we have 100 jobs on the cluster,, we are firing 3000 login request to the cluster to check the status of these jobs. This is not acceptable.

issue_title: DataSaver.add_result should fail with an error if unknown type is passed
issue_body:
Currently, if as part of a sweep, the result of an `ArrayParameter` is passed to `DataSaver.add_result` as a python list or tuple, as opposed to an `ndarray`, the data will silently be truncated to the first element. This is not made clear to the user and hence data is easily lost.

### Expected behaviour
Either an error should be thrown for an unknown type, or python sequence types should be handled.

### Actual behaviour
Data is silently truncated to the first item in the array.

### System
Windows on QCodes Master (940b46f6)

issue_title: [tests] Move some tests + consider using GNU Parallel for Jenkins?
issue_body:
### Describe the problem

Our testing/dev loop is pretty slow.

The Jenkins machines are more powerful than Travis (48 cores vs 2). 
Often times we are left waiting many hours for Travis tests to build, while the Jenkins tests execute almost instantly.

A lot of our Jenkins tests really only require 2 cores or so, so we can further optimize this by running these tests concurrently with something like GNU Parallel (which has [resource-based load balancing](https://www.gnu.org/software/parallel/parallel_tutorial.html#Limiting-the-resources))

I guess one of the big bottlenecks is just building the docker image in the first place, so we should maybe consider optimizing that.

issue_title: Support num_return_vals argument for actor methods.
issue_body:
Currently, we allow remote functions to return multiple object IDs using the following syntax.

```python
@ray.remote(num_return_vals=3)
def f():
    return 1, 2, 3
```

Then `f.remote()` returns

```
[ObjectID(071b78e52bbe1e2c14e89c823fa3662ba11ee264),
 ObjectID(041b78e52bbe1e2c14e89c823fa3662ba11ee264),
 ObjectID(051b78e52bbe1e2c14e89c823fa3662ba11ee264)]
```

However, there is no equivalent at the moment for actor methods, so actor methods always return a single object ID.

We should allow actor methods to return multiple object IDs. What should the syntax be (since we don't decorate the individual actor methods)? There are a couple options.

1. Add a decorator for actor methods that return multiple object IDs, e.g.,

    ```python
    @ray.remote
    class Foo(object):
        @ray.multiple_return_vals(3)
        def method(self):
            return 1, 2, 3
    ```

2. Make it an argument to the remote function invocation.

    ```python
    foo.method.remote(__ray_num_return_vals__=3)
    ```

issue_title: Make sure ARROW_LOG(FATAL) gives backtrace and also prints errno.
issue_body:
I've seen the plasma store die with no information at
https://github.com/apache/arrow/blob/c71a56527726f3cab58b37ad61a2129662f8a4fb/cpp/src/plasma/plasma.cc#L41.

It just prints

```
Failed to write message to client on fd 87.
```

This can happen when a driver disconnects, but we need to know what the errno is so we can handle it (as a separate issue, we may just want to allow all errno's here since new ones seem to keep arising).

issue_title: DOC: How to support input type='range' and other input types?
issue_body:
I have an `IntegerField` field in my model and a crispy form associated with an UpdateView to update it. The default view for an IntegerField in crispy is an `input type='number'` which is a super sensible default. However, I'd like to use a `range` instead since my `IntegerField` has a min/max validator, and a high min and even higher max, and so `input type='number'` isn't the ideal UI element. 

I'm new to crispy, and so the broader question I think is how to change how forms get rendered. I think the way might be with Layouts, but since I'm not actually trying to change the layout of my form, but instead change the form elements themselves, and I haven't seen anything in the Layout docs about alternate input types, I'm not sure how to proceed.

As requested, I [originally posted this question to stackoverflow](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/46634829/range-input-in-django-crispy-forms) but it wasn't getting too much action there so I hope you'll excuse me adding it here. 

As for this github issue, I think it can serve as a request to document the answer to the question I have for future crispy users. I'm happy to add those docs if you can help me answer this question here...

Thanks!

issue_title: Update Plugin requests policy
issue_body:
**I opened this so it won't go under.**

---

### Description

as discussed in https://github.com/streamlink/streamlink/issues/1763

The **Plugin requests** Guide should be updated with some rules about plugins.

https://github.com/streamlink/streamlink/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#plugin-requests

in https://github.com/streamlink/streamlink/issues/1763 were mentioned

- stolen content
- rehosted content
- NSFW sites

---

## My opinion

> stolen content / rehosted content

---

1. sites which **only focus** on stolen content should **not be added**

**Example:** _just a website with a lot of ads and some rehosted streams_

https://github.com/streamlink/streamlink/issues/603
https://github.com/streamlink/streamlink/issues/1207
https://github.com/streamlink/streamlink/issues/1243

---

2. sites where the **User takes advantage** of the streaming site,
but it's **not** the **main focus** of the website they **could be added**.

**Example:** _a lot of people abuse youtube/twitch for sports streams, there are some other similar sites where this happens._

https://github.com/streamlink/streamlink/issues/701
https://github.com/streamlink/streamlink/issues/1176

---

> VOD in general which I would not add

I for one don't care for vods on smaller websites,

https://github.com/streamlink/streamlink/issues/1321

or if they need a lot of changes in compare to the livestream

https://github.com/streamlink/streamlink/issues/1733

Website with only stolen vod content

https://github.com/streamlink/streamlink/issues/1110

---

And every new plugin request should have a explanation about the website which is requested,
sometimes a simple wikipedia link is enough.

If this is not given, the **plugin request should be closed** or in general if there are not details.

issue_title: Add support for `archive media` in API
issue_body:
Scrolling through the API, I notice that we have a function to delete a post, (`deleteMedia(self, mediaId)`). Instagram also offers the ability to archive a post, that is make it invisible for a limited or unlimited period of time.

**Archive** is very useful when one of your post has too many likes. You can archive it before boting and then unarchive it.

It is also very useful if you want to have some media to display only when you are boting a given set of hashtag... For instance, archive your nature media before boting against the `fashion` tag, and _vis versa_ (archive your fashion related posts when boting against the `nature` tag)

Anyone know how the support to archive a post and display archived posts can be implemented?

issue_title: item和pipelines能否结合一下DjangoItem，使用Django的Model功能
Fix compatibility with anaconda
lstm - pytorch exported pb doesn't match specification
issue_body:
Use Netron open onnx/backend/test/data/pytorch-operator/test_operator_lstm/model.onnx can get:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6838753/39824565-a4bf96d2-53ea-11e8-87c5-edb132a839e7.png)

Here, output from first LSTM should have shape [seq_length, num_directions, batch_size, hidden_size] following the docs. (In pytorch, should have  (seq_len, batch, hidden_size * num_directions))
And then pass to next nodes [Shape, Gather, Unsqueeze ....] to get second LSTM's initial_h and initial_c which has shape [num_directions, batch_size, hidden_size].

Here is the problem,
normally, Gather should gather batch_size from output, and Concat with num_directions and hidden_size to obtain [num_directions, batch_size, hidden_size].
But pytorch export pb with index 1 of Gather and get num_directions because output shape changed from (seq_len, batch, hidden_size * num_directions) to [seq_length, num_directions, batch_size, hidden_size]. batch_size is no longer at index 1.
Finally get [num_directions, num_directions, hidden_size] to make a wrong initial_h and initial_c for second LSTM.

Any help would be much appreciated.

issue_title: RDFa parser fails on typed literals when xml:lang is specified
issue_body:
vfaronov, 2010-02-09T21:18:39.000Z 
What steps will reproduce the problem?
1. Prepare an XHTML+RDFa document in which there are both an element
producing a typed literal, and an xml:lang specification (e.g. on the root
html element). An example document is attached.
2. Feed this file to the RDFLib parser.

What is the expected output? What do you see instead?
The expected output is one triple in which the object is a typed literal,
as per "RDFa in XHTML: Syntax and Processing" section 6.3.1.2. Instead,
RDFLib reports "TypeError: A Literal can only have one of lang or datatype,
per http://www.w3.org/TR/rdf-concepts/#section-Graph-Literal"

What version of the product are you using? On what operating system?
I am using RDFLib trunk (r1843) on Debian GNU/Linux 5.0.

Please provide any additional information below.
The issue exists because the parser passes the current lang value to the
Literal() constructor even though the datatype is specified. The
constructor then complains.

Comment 1 by eik...@gmail.com



Comment 2 by vfaronov
The attached patch fixes the issue for me without causing TC #108 to fail.


Comment 3 by eik...@gmail.com
Fixed in r1842. Thank you for reporting the issue and supplying the patch.


Comment 4 by eik...@gmail.com
The next release is going to be the 3.0 release.


Comment 5 by vfaronov
eikeon, this issue has already been fixed in r1845. It just wasn't marked as fixed
for some reason.


Comment 6 by eik...@gmail.com
vfaronov, thank you. Marking as fixed now.
